text:
New Zealand's Lydia Ko won gold in the women's Olympic golf competition on Saturday, holding her nerve in a tense finish to complete her medal set in her final Games.Ko held off a late charge from Germany's Esther Henseleit, who claimed silver two strokes behind, with China's Lin Xiyu a further shot back to take bronze.The 27-year-old New Zealander, a two-time major winner and former world number one who first topped the rankings aged 17, was chasing gold to add to her silver in Rio and bronze in Tokyo.South Korean-born Ko started the final round as co-leader with Morgane Metraux of Switzerland and was cruising towards victory as she advanced to 11 under after her front nine holes to establish a five-stroke lead over Henseleit.But a wayward shot on the 13th found the water en route to a double bogey, opening the door for Henseleit who cut Ko's lead to one stroke.Playing in the final group, Ko kept her nerve with a string of par putts before slotting in a birdie on the 18th green to bag gold on 10 under par."I just stayed patient and stuck to my game plan, and you know, 18, that tee shot and the second shot was probably two of the most important shots in my life," she told reporters after the medal ceremony in which she shed tears on the podium.Paris will be her final Games, she said after the victory which secures her entry into women's golf LPGA Hall of Fame.Henseleit's superb fourth-round performance, the second-best score of the day, yielded a first Olympic women's golf medal for Germany and Europe.For China, Lin Xiyu, who also calls herself Janet Lin, is the country's second women's golf medallist after Feng Shanshan, who won bronze in Rio.Metraux slumped to tied 18th at two under par, failing to recover from two bogeys and a triple bogey in her first five holes. The Swiss Olympic debutant said dizziness during the round contributed to a tough day.Defending champion and world number one Nelly Korda of the United States fell out of medal contention during the final round to end at one under par in tied 22nd.

Stephanie Meadow and Leona Maguire were never in medal contention, but both recorded their best scores of the week in the final round.Meadow carded a closing 70 to finish 39th on six over, while Maguire's 71 resulted in an overall score of 24 over as she finished 59th and last of those to complete the tournament.

"If it was any other week I probably wouldn't have played but it's the Olympic Games." - Leona Maguire reveals that illness limited her on the golf course this week📺 Live on@RTE2and@RTEPlayerhttps://t.co/b2OI4OoWVJ📱 Updateshttps://t.co/7SCMElSayb#Paris2024#Olympicspic.twitter.com/S2RijnNWgp

However, the Cavan woman has been battling illness all week, and told RTÉ Sport after her round: "I've felt absolutely rotten the last few days.

"I know that wasn't the golf that I was capable of, and it was one of those weeks. If it was any other week, I probably wouldn’t have played, but it’s the Olympic games and, representing Ireland, there’s no bigger honour.

"It’s one of those things you don’t give up on until the very end, even if it feels too far gone. I suppose it was more a point to prove to myself today that I knew I could handle this golf course.

"Under par on this golf course, I’ll take that any day."

Maguire paid tribute to the support she had received from Irish fans at Le Golf National and Team Ireland, with the world number 30 adding: "They've been fantastic, these are some of the biggest crowds we’ve ever seen, so many tricolours, so many jerseys.

"If it wasn’t for all the support, I probably would have thought about packing it in. But I think it’s phenomenal, the whole support the Irish team has got both at home and over here the last few weeks.

"It’s been incredible and the support from Team Ireland, in general, there’s been so many people working behind the scenes to help us athletes.

"The medal count isn’t an accident. There’s been a lot of people doing a lot of work behind the scenes. I'm very grateful to be part of a great team."

title: Paris 2024: Olympic bridesmaid Ko strikes golf gold
description: New Zealand's Lydia Ko won gold in the women's Olympic golf competition, holding her nerve in a tense finish to complete her medal set in her final Games, while Ireland team-mates Stephanie Meadow and Leona Maguire never threatened to land a blow.
